mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS exceeds +0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering rapid equity sell-offs within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.5%, or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms robust consumer demand, driving sustained corporate earnings growth and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive mean return over 60 days in >60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in quarterly regression models.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates supply-side contraction that outweighs demand, forcing negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines produce positive 20-day returns in >58% of cases during tightening periods, or the mechanism shows no directional bias when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 3.2% forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 330, or the threshold effect vanishes in a walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with falling unemployment rates creates a tight labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 60-day returns in >55% of instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts indicate increased construction activity and durable goods demand, which translates to stronger corporate revenues and positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: HOUST increases correlate with negative 20-day returns in >60% of observations, or the series shows a Rank IC below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals persistent supply chain bottlenecks, triggering rapid equity sell-offs within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >57% of instances following a >0.5% INDPRO drop during recovery phases, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.
